text: لَمَّا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ الْعَقْلَ قَالَ لَهُ أَقْبِلْ فَأَقْبَلَ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ أَدْبِرْ فَأَدْبَرَ فَقَالَ وَ عِزَّتِي وَ جَلَالِي مَا خَلَقْتُ خَلْقاً أَحْسَنَ مِنْكَ إِيَّاكَ آمُرُ وَ إِيَّاكَ أَنْهَى وَ إِيَّاكَ أُثِيبُ وَ إِيَّاكَ أُعَاقِبُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Abu Najran, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Created the intellect, Said to it: “Come!” So it came. Then Said: “Turn back!” So it turned back. So He<sup>azwj</sup> Said: “By My<sup>azwj</sup> Might and My<sup>azwj</sup> Majesty! I<sup>azwj</sup> have not Created a creature better than you. To you I<sup>azwj</sup> shall Command, and to you I<sup>azwj</sup> shall Forbid, and to you<sup>azwj</sup> shall Reward, and to you I<sup>azwj</sup> shall Punish”’.
